methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $37M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Biotech & Health (found 0 same-stage, 2 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 8 same-sector peers exist in Biotech & Health, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 6.9x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health

data.narrative:
Nabla Bio is a Series A-stage biotech company founded by Surge Biswas and Frances Anastassacos. The company has raised $37M to date alongside $1.5B+ in partnerships. The valuation analysis is informed by a peer universe of 3 public companies in Biotech & Health (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L) and 8 private peers including Colossal Biosciences, Abridge, and Recursion Pharmaceuticals.

Of three valuation methods tested, only stage-progression analysis proved applicable. Using total capital raised of $37M as a proxy for current valuation and applying a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A to next-stage progression yields an implied valuation of $74M. Peer-median triangulation could not be executed due to insufficient narrow-stage comparables—while 8 same-sector peers exist, none met the threshold of three datapoints at matching stage, adjacent stage, or similar valuation tier. Public-comp anchoring using the sector's 6.9x median price-to-sales multiple across the three public tickers is not feasible absent revenue disclosure, which is standard for private companies.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $63M to $85M, centered on $74M. This range reflects the methodological constraint that only one triangulation point is available. Key uncertainties include the company's actual revenue or comparable metrics, which would enable public-comp anchoring and narrow peer selection. Partnership revenue recognition and burn dynamics remain unmapped.
